school_objects.meaning: A bag you wear on your back to carry school things
school_objects.example: My blue backpack holds my lunch box, books, and pencils.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A backpack is a bag you wear to carry your school stuff.

school_objects.meaning: A tool for writing and drawing that can be erased
school_objects.example: I use my yellow pencil to write my name on the paper.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A pencil is what you write with at school.

school_objects.meaning: A small object that removes pencil marks
school_objects.example: My eraser has a unicorn on it and smells like grapes.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: An eraser rubs away pencil mistakes.

school_objects.meaning: A stick of colored wax for drawing
school_objects.example: I use a red crayon to draw a heart for my mom.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Crayons are colorful sticks for drawing pictures.

school_objects.meaning: A pen with a thick tip and bright ink
school_objects.example: The purple marker is my favorite for coloring flowers.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Markers are bright pens for coloring.

school_objects.meaning: A straight tool for measuring and drawing lines
school_objects.example: My ruler is 12 inches long and has a bumpy edge.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A ruler measures how long something is.

school_objects.meaning: A tool with two blades for cutting paper
school_objects.example: I use scissors carefully to cut out a star shape.

school_objects.meaning: A sticky substance that makes things stick together
school_objects.example: I put glue on the back of the picture and stick it to the paper.

school_objects.meaning: A book of blank paper for writing
school_objects.example: My notebook has lines to help me write neatly.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A notebook is where you write in school.

school_objects.meaning: A folded piece of cardboard that holds papers
school_objects.example: I keep my homework in my red folder so I don't lose it.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A folder holds your papers together.

school_objects.meaning: A container that holds your lunch food
school_objects.example: My lunchbox has a dinosaur on it and keeps my sandwich cold.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A lunchbox carries your food to school.

school_objects.example: I fill my water bottle at the fountain when I am thirsty.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A water bottle holds your water.

school_objects.meaning: A small machine that does math problems
school_objects.example: I use my calculator to check if 5 plus 3 equals 8.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A calculator helps you do math.

school_objects.meaning: A round model of the Earth
school_objects.example: The globe spins so I can see all the countries.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A globe is a round map of the world.

school_objects.meaning: A tool that makes pencils pointy again
school_objects.example: My pencil is dull, so I put it in the sharpener.
